name: Coconut Joys
recipeInstructions:
Preparation time: 30 minutes Chilling time: 1 hour
1. Melt butter in a saucepan over low heat. Remove from heat and stir in the confectioners' sugar, flaked coconut, and milk until well combined.
2. Using rounded teaspoons, shape small balls of the mixture on a cookie sheet lined with wax paper. Make a slight indentation in the center of each ball.
3. Fill each indentation with melted chocolate.
4. Chill the cookies until firm (about 1 hour). Store them in the refrigerator. These freeze well and look beautiful arranged on a serving tray.
Winner Shere Case of Hickory Hills describes her cookie entry:
"Holiday preparation usually starts in September and continues until the night before Christmas. I have been doing this for almost 18 years, and each year I add more recipes. This particular cookie, passed down to me by my sister-in-law, Diana Forkash from Wisconsin, is among the favorites of family and friends. It looks absolutely gorgeous on a plate, tastes wonderful, and is absolutely the easiest thing to make. There is no baking time involved; you can sit down and enjoy making them."
